name: Badr ibn Ma'qil
title: Lion of Ju'fi
shortDescription: A brave man from the Ju'fi tribe who rose to defend the Master of Martyrs on Ashura and was martyred in the first attack.
fullStory:
Badr ibn Ma'qil al-Ju'fi was a known warrior and hero from the Ju'fi tribe. With a heart full of love for the Ahl al-Bayt, he reached Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan despite the numerous dangers.

On the day of Ashura, with the start of Umar Sa'd's army's cowardly all-out attack in the morning (known as the first attack), Badr ibn Ma'qil, like the other dedicated companions of the Imam, drew his sword.

Possessing exemplary bravery, he stood shoulder to shoulder with his religious brothers against the flood of Kufan soldiers. After a heavy battle and taking down a number of enemies, he attained the grace of martyrdom in that very first attack.

His name is mentioned in authentic ziyarat texts such as Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa, which shows his high status with the Ahl al-Bayt (AS).
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى بَدْرِ بْنِ مَعْقِلِ الجُعْفِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Badr ibn Ma'qil al-Ju'fi.
